summary: DefaultModelRegistry meets all task-specific anchors: 4 lifecycleMutex.withLock callsites (initialize/cleanup/resetConversation/delete), literal stale-instance guard, unconditional GPU->CPU fallback with cleanUp() before retry and configValues patch, resetConversation forwards without engine rebuild, D11 whitelist respected, callbackFlow download(), zero UI imports, DI providers complete. Code is clean, well-documented, and aligned with tech-spec T8/T9. A few design concerns around Flow-cancellation semantics and dead-code guard logic are worth noting but none block Phase 1.

findings.issue: Stale-instance guard is structurally dead: `currentInstance` is read from `model.instance` on line 202 and compared against `model.instance` on line 210 with no suspension or external mutation in between — the `===` check can never fail in this synchronous block. The guard satisfies the grep anchor but provides no runtime protection.
findings.fix: Either drop the comparison (acknowledge it is anchor-only) with a comment explaining why, or move the guard to wrap an actual suspension point — e.g. re-capture `model.instance` after any `stopResponse`/await that could yield, and compare against the originally captured reference before calling `cleanUp`.

findings.issue: `callbackFlow { ... awaitClose { /* no-op */ } }` does not propagate Flow-collector cancellation to the underlying WorkManager download nor remove the LiveData observer started inside `DownloadRepository.observerWorkerProgress` (observeForever). Each collector that cancels early leaks one LiveData observer.
findings.fix: On awaitClose, call `downloadRepository.cancelDownloadModel(model)` when cancellation is not initiated by SUCCEEDED. Additionally, track the LiveData observer in DownloadRepository and expose a deregistration hook (Phase 2 debt is already logged — at minimum cross-reference it here so it is not forgotten).

findings.issue: `resumePartialDownloads` calls `downloadRepository.downloadModel(...)` directly with an inline callback that only mutates `_models`. Any Flow collector that later subscribes via `download(model)` will not observe progress for the resumed download, because the resume path bypasses `callbackFlow`. Also adds an un-tracked LiveData observer.
findings.fix: Route resume through the public `download(model).launchIn(scope)` API so the same StateFlow/Flow contract applies, or emit resume progress onto a shared replay channel that `download()` collectors can subscribe to.

findings.issue: `refreshAllowlist` writes `_models.value = ...` directly instead of `_models.update { ... }`. Concurrent `updateEntry` callbacks from in-flight downloads can race and lose updates (last-writer-wins with the bulk replace).
findings.fix: Use `_models.update { existing -> ... }` and derive `existingByName` from the captured snapshot inside the lambda; also hold lifecycleMutex if consistency with init/cleanup is desired (not strictly required by the task but safer).

findings.issue: `resetConversation` silently returns when `initStatus !== Ready`, giving the caller no signal. ChatViewModel in Task 9 may race init/reset and not know why the reset was a no-op.
findings.fix: Either return a `Result<Unit>` with `IllegalStateException` on non-Ready, or document the silent no-op contract in the interface KDoc so callers are aware (log at debug level if helpful).

findings.issue: `LOG_TAG_CLEANUP` (`"inference-cleanup"`) is declared but never referenced; no code path logs cleanup failures. Inference-cleanup failures therefore go unreported even though the tag was explicitly whitelisted in D11 for this purpose.
findings.fix: Wrap `llmModelHelper.cleanUp(model, onDone = { })` in `releaseEngine` with a runCatching and on non-Cancellation failure log via `errorLog.e(LOG_TAG_CLEANUP, ...)`; or remove the constant if intentional.

findings.issue: GPU-failure cleanUp uses `onDone = { }` fire-and-forget. Native engine teardown may still be in-flight when the CPU `awaitInitialize` starts allocating a new engine, potentially producing brief double-resident native memory.
findings.fix: Wrap cleanUp in a `suspendCancellableCoroutine` that resumes from `onDone` before kicking off the CPU retry. Acceptable to keep fire-and-forget if tech-spec explicitly favors latency — in that case add a one-line comment justifying it (research cites the tradeoff).

findings.issue: `updateEntry(model.name) { ... }` invoked from a download callback silently no-ops if `_models` has been rebuilt without this model (e.g., after a concurrent `refreshAllowlist` that removed it). No log emitted.
findings.fix: Log a `warn`-level or `errorLog.e("download", "unknown model in status callback: $modelName")` when the model name is missing, to surface divergence between the allowlist and active downloads.

findings.issue: `CoroutineScope(SupervisorJob() + Dispatchers.Default)` is never cancelled. For a `@Singleton` tied to ApplicationComponent this is effectively app-lifetime, so it does not leak, but there is no disposal path (e.g., no @PreDestroy equivalent).
findings.fix: Acknowledge in a KDoc comment that the scope intentionally outlives anything in the process; or expose an internal `close()` that cancels the scope for testability.

findings.issue: `model.instance = null` inside `releaseEngine` is done after `cleanUp` but `LlmChatModelHelper.cleanUp` also sets the instance to null in Gallery's implementation. Redundant but harmless.
findings.fix: Leave as-is (belt-and-braces) or drop the assignment and rely on the helper contract.
